exemplar.title: Investigating the effect of organic and inorganic fertilizer influence the sugar concentration on Long beans (Vigna sinensis L)

detailedExemplarData.title: Investigating the effect of organic and inorganic fertilizer influence the sugar concentration on Long beans (Vigna sinensis L)

subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Research question is situated within a specific agronomic and physiological context, detailing fertilizer types, quantitative levels and a clear response variable.
Comprehensive background on black teaâs bioactive compounds provides a strong mechanistic justification for its inclusion.
Methodology description includes masses, volumes, watering schedules and timing, allowing reproduction with few ambiguities.
Preliminary test rationale and variable identification show thoughtful methodological planning.
weaknesses: Literature review is at times overly expansive and could be more tightly focused on the core variables.
Apparatus list omits soil pH measurement equipment, an important factor in nutrient availability.
Leafâsampling criteria (age, node position) are not specified, risking variability in measurements.
Preliminary experiment concentrations are ambiguously reported (typographical errors), hindering exact reproducibility.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates a high level of contextualisation (Band 5) and a detailed, largely reproducible methodology (Band 4 for both considerations and description). Minor omissions in apparatus and sampling details prevent a perfect score. Overall, a robust design merits 5/6.

strengths: Raw and processed data are clearly organized in tables and bar graphs with error bars, supporting readability.
Means, standard deviations and a twoâway ANOVA are correctly calculated and appropriately applied.
Uncertainties (instrument precision) are stated alongside data, demonstrating attention to measurement error.
Discussion of error bar overlap and ANOVA interactions shows solid interpretation of statistical results.
weaknesses: Some table headings contain cluttered symbols and one table is partially garbled, compromising precision.
Uncertainty propagation into processed data and ANOVA is omitted, with error bars representing SD rather than measurement uncertainty.
Qualitative observations (Benedictâs test) are not quantitatively recorded; placeholder text is used instead of actual data.
No justification is provided for the chosen sample size (three replicates), weakening the statistical rigor.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Communication of data is clear but not fully precise (Band 3â4), evidence of uncertainties is present but with omissions (Band 3), and data processing is largely accurate (Band 5). These mixed performances average to a solid 4/6.

strengths: Conclusion clearly states differential effects of NPK and tea powder on Brix %, directly supported by numerical results and ANOVA significance.
Discussion integrates multiple peerâreviewed sources on nutrient effects and allelopathy, providing relevant scientific comparisons.
The conclusion is fully consistent with the analysis and ties back to both raw data trends and statistical findings.
Justifications reference both means and interaction effects, demonstrating depth of interpretation.
weaknesses: Minor stylistic and structural issues in wording reduce overall precision.
At times the contextual comparison veers into overâlong digressions rather than concise synthesis.
Could more directly highlight which statistical contrasts (postâhoc) underpin the stated differences.
Limited mention of realâworld implications beyond the immediate experimental context.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion is both relevant and fully consistent with the analyzed data (Band 5â6) and makes substantial, justified comparisons to the scientific context (Band 5â6). Minor stylistic issues place it at 5/6.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (soil pH variability, environmental factors, seed quality) are identified and their potential impacts described.
Each identified weakness is paired with realistic, targeted improvements (greenhouse control, standardised seed selection, composting procedures).
The risk assessment section is comprehensive, demonstrating strong safety and ethical planning.
Improvements are relevant to the weaknesses and show a clear understanding of experimental refinement.
weaknesses: The relative impact or prioritisation of the weaknesses is not fully explained, limiting depth of evaluation.
Feasibility details (cost, time, equipment) for proposed improvements are not discussed.
No quantification or assessment of how much each improvement would reduce specific errors.
Seed quality control could be further strengthened by recommending viability or vigor assays rather than mass matching alone.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The evaluation describes specific weaknesses and realistic improvements (Band 3â4) but stops short of explaining their relative impact or detailed feasibility (Band 3â4). Overall, this merits 4/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student states that increasing NPK raises Brix% while increasing tea powder lowers it and links this directly to the numerical means and ANOVA significance. The conclusion is **fully consistent with, and explicitly justified by, the analysed data**, fulfilling the 5-6 descriptor. Minor stylistic issues prevent awarding the very top; hence 5.

Raw data are presented in clearly structured tables and supported by bar graphs with error bars; processed data (means, SDs) are tabulated neatly. Some headings contain clutter (unicode symbols) and Table 3 is partially garbled, so **precision is compromised even though clarity is maintained**. Meeting one of the two qualities places the work in band 3-4; given overall readability a score of 4 is appropriate.

Means, standard deviations and a two-way ANOVA are appropriate and correctly executed (spot-checks confirm mean calculations; ANOVA table structure is sound). Graphs include trendlines and error bars that match processed data, indicating **largely accurate processing**. Minor typographic errors in the black-tea table do not alter computed results, so the overall quality meets the âappropriately and accuratelyâ descriptor; small transcription issues preclude the very top of the band, giving 5.

Four concrete weaknesses (soil pH variation, outdoor conditions, seed quality, germination inhibition) are **described with some explanation of how each could distort results**, e.g. nutrient imbalance affecting sugar accumulation. The analysis stops short of quantifying or prioritising relative impact, so the strand fits the descriptor âdescribedâ rather than âexplainedâ, justifying the top of the 3-4 band (4).

The discussion compares findings with multiple peer-reviewed sources on nutrient effects, allelopathy and sugar transport, integrating them to justify observed trends, thereby providing a **relevant and reasoned comparison to accepted scientific context**. The breadth of citations shows depth, but occasional over-long digressions reduce precision; accordingly, 5 marks within the 5-6 band are appropriate.

The research question is framed with clear reference to two specific fertilizer types, defined quantitative levels, the target organism (Vigna sinensis) and the response variable (Brix %). Extensive background paragraphs outline why sugar content is agriculturally relevant and how NPK versus black-tea nutrients might influence it. This places the question within a **specific agronomic and physiological context rather than a merely general one**. Minor digressions in the literature review do not detract from the fact that the context is appropriate and detailed, justifying placement in the 5-6 band; the articulation, while thorough, lacks the tight concision that would merit the very top of the band, so 5 is awarded.

Instrument uncertainties (Â±0.01 g, Â±0.1 Brix, etc.) are stated in the apparatus list and quoted in data tables, showing **attention to uncertainty**. However, there is no propagation of these uncertainties into the means or in the ANOVA, and error bars represent SD rather than measurement uncertainty. These omissions are âsignificant inaccuraciesâ, so the strand sits in band 3-4 but nearer the lower end; 3 marks are awarded.

Each weakness is paired with a realistic, relevant improvement (hydroponics, greenhouse, seed mass standardisation, gradual composting). The student **describes** how these address the stated issues but does not detail mechanisms or feasibility cost-benefit, so the work aligns with band 3-4. Clarity and direct linkage to weaknesses justify awarding 4 marks.

The student identifies variables, gives a rationale for concentration increments based on a preliminary test, and recognises risks, ethics and several environmental factors, showing **thoughtful consideration of data relevancy and sufficiency**. However, the discussion stops at listing rather than fully **explaining how each consideration will secure reliable, sufficient data** (e.g. why three replicates are enough, or how long 30 days ensures steady-state sugar levels). Consequently the work fits the descriptor "described" rather than "explained", justifying the upper end of the 3-4 band (score 4).

Step-by-step procedures include masses, volumes, watering schedules and timing, enabling another investigator to **reasonably reproduce the work**. Ambiguities remain (e.g. leaf age/position for sampling, exact calibration procedure for the refractometer, outdoor light conditions), but these are few enough that replication is still plausible with only minor interpretation. This satisfies the âreproduced with few ambiguitiesâ descriptor, meriting 4 in the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: The background on black teaâs bioactive compounds and allelochemicals is comprehensive and illuminates mechanisms that justify its selection as an experimental variable.
In the NPK description, ensure correct formatting of chemical notation and citations; replace inline LaTeX escape sequences with standardized text to improve readability.

The research question is well-defined within a specific agronomic and physiological context, detailing two fertilizer types, quantitative levels, and a clear response variableâexemplifying excellent contextual grounding.

The introduction contains extensive background but could be more focused; tighten literature review to more directly link each point to the research questionâs core variables for concision and relevance.
The apparatus list omits a pH meter or soil testing kit; including soil pH measurement is vital given pHâs known influence on nutrient availability.
Specify leaf sampling criteria (leaf age, node position) to reduce variability and ensure consistency in sugar measurements across samples.
For the Benedictâs test, provide a quantitative colorimetric scale or spectrophotometric measurement instead of visual observation to increase precision.
The risk assessment comprehensively addresses chemical, mechanical, and biological hazards, reflecting strong experimental safety planning.
The qualitative data table uses placeholder text for images rather than actual observations; transcribe observed color changes to convey meaningful data.
Table captions should match table numbering sequentially and include concise titles; adjust âTable 1. Qualitative dataâ to reflect its position correctly.
The section on Vigna sinensis clearly outlines species characteristics and nutritional relevance, effectively contextualizing the organism within the study.
The preliminary experiment description has ambiguous concentration ranges and typographical errors (â(1g, 2g, 1g)3g,4g,5gâ); clarify and correct these values to ensure reproducibility.

The rationale for independent variable increments would benefit from a deeper explanation of how preliminary germination outcomes informed the final concentrations, enhancing methodological justification.
The choice of three replicates per treatment lacks justification; include a brief power or error analysis to explain why this sample size is sufficient.
Controlled watering volumes are precisely specified (50 mL every two days), demonstrating careful control over potential confounding factors.
Simplify the header âsucrose (Â± 0.1 Brix) percentage mass of NPK fertilizerâ to âBrix % (Â± 0.1) for NPK concentrationsâ for greater clarity.
The black tea data table contains unclear LaTeX symbols (e.g., \overline{A}, Î¸); standardize entries to numerical values to avoid confusion.
Quantitative raw data are neatly organized in table form, facilitating clear communication of the Brix measurements across trials.
Clarify whether population (N) or sample (n â 1) standard deviation is intended and adjust the formula to match the datasetâs replication scheme.
Processed data include correctly calculated means and standard deviations for NPK treatments, demonstrating accurate data analysis.
The processed mean and standard deviation for black tea treatments are clearly presented, supporting transparent comparison.
When summarizing data trends, reference the variability (SD) to contextualize the reliability of mean comparisons, enhancing the narrative depth.
Annotate Graph 1 with significance indicators (e.g., asterisks for p < 0.05) to visually convey statistical findings directly on the graph.
The discussion of error bar overlap in Graphs 1 and 2 shows appropriate consideration of uncertainties, linking statistical interpretation to visual data.
The two-way ANOVA description clearly explains factor and interaction effects, demonstrating advanced data processing techniques.
Describe any post-hoc tests performed (e.g., Tukeyâs HSD) to identify which treatment pairs differ significantly, strengthening the statistical conclusions.
The evaluation articulates specific methodological weaknesses (soil pH, nutrient variability) and their potential impacts, aligning well with the rubricâs evaluation criteria.
The proposed greenhouse or growth chamber for environmental control is a realistic improvement directly addressing uncontrolled outdoor variables.
To further refine seed quality control, incorporate seed viability assays or vigor tests rather than relying solely on mass matching.
Include a brief feasibility discussion of composting tea powder (time, equipment) to demonstrate a comprehensive understanding of proposed improvements.
Bibliographic entries show inconsistent formatting and missing DOI information; standardize the citation style (e.g., APA or MLA) and include persistent identifiers for completeness.
